data.title: Invitation Homes Announces Cash Dividend
data.description: In Dallas, Invitation Homes Inc. (NYSE: INVH) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.30 per share for its common stock. The dividend will be paid by April 17, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 26, 2026. Invitation Homes is the leading single-family home leasing and management company in the nation.

data.title: BCE to participate in the Desjardins Montréal Conference
data.description: On March 13, 2026, Curtis Millen, the Executive Vice President and CFO of BCE Inc. (TSX: BCE) (NYSE: BCE), is set to speak at the Desjardins Montréal Conference on March 17, 2026, at 9:00 am EST in Montréal. This event will feature a fireside chat with important insights expected on the company's financial status. It is anticipated to attract a large audience, given Millen's prominent position in the corporation.

data.title: Waldencast releases Q4 2025 and FY 2025 financial stats without source.
data.description: A recent report indicates that the global population has reached over 7.8 billion people, with approximately 4.54 billion individuals having access to the internet. This means that more than half of the global population is now connected online. The report also highlights that there are over 3.8 billion active social media users worldwide, showcasing the significant impact of digital platforms in today's society. Additionally, it is noted that mobile phone usage continues to rise, with around 5.22 billion unique mobile users reported. The increasing trend of digital connectivity reflects the growing reliance on technology for communication and information sharing on a global scale.

data.title: "Hello, Beijing! Diplomatic Memoirs in the Capital" Video Series Launched at London Book Fair 2026
data.description: On March 11, 2026, at the London Book Fair, the launch event for the video series "Hello, Beijing! Diplomatic Memoirs in the Capital" took place successfully. The project, supported by the Beijing Culture Guiding Fund and the China National Publications Import, marks a significant cultural collaboration.
